member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.Interop.NativeTest.Initialize(System.String,System.Boolean,System.IntPtr@,System.IntPtr@,System.Guid@,System.Int64@)
member.summary: Initialize test environment, create replica, and calls OpenReplica automatically, supports recovery modes to be sepecified.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.Interop.NativeTest.ResetReplica(System.IntPtr@)
member.summary: Close the replica and create a new one. You need to call OpenReplica afterwards - before hooking up any necessary StateManager events

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.Interop.NativeTest.OpenReplica
member.summary: Opens the replica and switch to primary

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.Interop.NativeTest.RequestCheckpointAfterNextTransaction
member.summary: Trigger checkpoint after next transaction. Useful in rebuilt event testing / backup and recovery testing

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.Interop.StateProviderInfo.FromStateProvider(Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.TraceId,System.IntPtr,System.Uri,Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.Interop.StateProviderKind)
member.summary: Retrieve StateProvider information from a state provider and does necessary validation

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.Interop.StateProviderInfo.GetSimpleTypeName(System.String)
member.summary:
Transforms type names from assembly qualified format to a simpler format for serialization.
Transforms type names from: - Namespace.Foo`1[[System.Int32, mscorlib, Version=4.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b77a5c561934e089]], MyAssembly, Version=1.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=null to: - Namespace.Foo`1[[System.Int32, mscorlib]], MyAssembly
member.returns: Type name usable for replicated serialization.

member.name: F:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.Interop.StateProviderKind.TStore
member.summary: ReliableDictionary

member.name: F:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.Interop.StateProviderKind.ConcurrentQueue
member.summary: ReliableConcurrentQueue

member.name: F:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.Interop.StateProviderKind.ReliableDictionary_Compat
member.summary: ReliableDictionary C# compat mode

member.name: T:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.NativeWrapper
member.summary: Base of native wrapper objects based on handles For now this only provides Ownership support for a parent object (say ReliableDictionaryImpl) owning the underlying native wrapper (say TStore)

member.name: F:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.NativeWrapper._owner
member.summary: The owner of this NativeWrapper. Typically used when you have a managed object wrapping a native wrapper and want to maintain identity in this field.

member.name: F:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.NativeWrapper._ownsHandle
member.summary: Whether this native wrapper owns the handle In some cases, you want to create a "weak" reference that have a implicit lifetime

member.name: F:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.NativeWrapper._handle
member.summary: The native handle

member.name: P:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.NativeWrapper.NativeHandle
member.summary: Returns the native handle

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.NativeWrapper.DisposeInternal(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Dispose implementation

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.NativeWrapper.ReleaseHandle(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Release the underlying native handle

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.NativeWrapper.DisposeImpl(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Rest of the dispose implementation - up to the derived class This is always called regardless whether OwnsHandle is true/false NOTE: This is guaranteed to get called once either in Dispose or in finalization, and isDisposing is set accordingly

member.name: P:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.NativeWrapper.Owner
member.summary: Owner of this TStore object. Useful for binding identity of this Tstore to a Dictionary implementation so that we could avoid having two identity cache both for ReliableDictionaryImpl and TStore

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.NativeWrapper.GetOrCreateOwner(System.Func{System.Object})
member.summary: Returns the associated owner if there is already one, or create a new one. Typically used when you have a managed object wrapping a native wrapper and want to maintain 1:1 mapping
member.param: The callback that creates a new owner instances. This may gets called more than once in races.

member.name: T:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.ReliableIdentityCache`1
member.summary: Identity cache for native wrapper types based on handle

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.ReliableIdentityCache`1.GetOrCreate(System.IntPtr@,System.Func{System.IntPtr,`0})
member.summary: Get or create a new object.
member.param: Native handle to the native object with a ref count. There are two cases: 1) We already have a wrapper for this handle in the cache. pHandle is not touched. 2) We need to create a new wrapper, which "consumes" the AddRef as needed. CreateCallback is then called to create the object. Once the object is created successfully, pHandle is immediately NULLed-out.
Callback to be called to create the object handle as needed.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.ReliableIdentityCache`1.Add(System.IntPtr,`0)
member.summary: Add into internal cache This should only be used when there is structural guarantee that there won't be conflict (such as when you are creating a new transaction)

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.ReliableIdentityCache`1.Get(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Return existing item. If the key isn't present or it is present but the item is being finalized, return null

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.ReliableIdentityCache`1.Remove(System.IntPtr,System.Object)
member.summary: Remove from cache only when toRemove matches the item in the dictionary Otherwise no-op

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Common.ReliableIdentityCache`1.ClearAll
member.summary: Clear everything in the cache and call Dispose This usually needed during shutdown

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.IStateSerializerResolver.GetStateSerializer``1(System.Uri)
member.summary: Get state serializer.
Returns the cached transaction based on the handle or create a new transaction instance
member.param: Transactional replicator to use for transaction
Reliable identity cache to use for transaction
member.returns: Existing transaction if found. A new transaction object otherwise.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.TransactionalReplicator.GetTStore``1(System.IntPtr@,System.Uri,Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.IStateSerializer{``0})
member.summary: Get or create a new instance of TStore based on store handle as identity, if useIdentityCache = true Otherwise, this always creates a new instance the store handle that comes in with a RefCount. GetTStore will null it out when it succeeds, if typedStateProviderCache not defined

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.TransactionalReplicator.GetTConcurrentQueue``1(System.IntPtr@,System.Uri,Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.IStateSerializer{``0})
member.summary: Get or create a new instance of TConcurrentQueue based on store handle as identity, if useIdentityCache = true Otherwise, this always creates a new instance the store handle that come in with a RefCount. GetTConcurrentQueue will null it out when it succeeds

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.TransactionalReplicator.GetTStore``1(System.IntPtr,System.Uri,Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.IStateSerializer{``0})
member.summary: Get or create a new instance of TStore based on store handle as identity, if useIdentityCache = true Otherwise, this always creates a new instance the store handle that come in without a RefCount. GetTStore will increment refcount if new TStore instance is created

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.TransactionalReplicator.GetTConcurrentQueue``1(System.IntPtr,System.Uri,Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.IStateSerializer{``0})
member.summary: Get or create a new instance of TConcurrentQueue based on store handle as identity, if useIdentityCache = true Otherwise, this always creates a new instance the store handle that comes in without a RefCount. GetTConcurrentQueue will increment refcount if new TStore instance is created

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.TransactionalReplicator.GetReliableState(System.IntPtr@,System.Uri,Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.IStateSerializerResolver)
member.summary: Get or create a new instance of ReliableState based on stateProvider handle as identity, if useIdentityCache = true Otherwise, this always creates a new instance the stateProvider handle that come in with a RefCount. It will be null'd on return when api succeeds

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.TransactionalReplicator.GetReliableState(System.IntPtr,System.Uri,Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.IStateSerializerResolver)
member.summary: Get or create a new instance of ReliableState based on stateProvider handle as identity, if useIdentityCache = true Otherwise, this always creates a new instance the stateProvider handle that come in without a RefCount. It's refcount will be incremented if new instance is created

member.name: T:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Store.KeyValueTuple`1
member.summary: Structure defines a key value tuple for the TStore. The version sequence number is used to track the version of the key value tuple.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Store.KeyValueTuple`1.#ctor(System.String,`0,System.Int64)
member.summary: Key value tuple constructor

member.name: P:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Store.KeyValueTuple`1.Key
member.summary: Key of the key value tuple.

member.name: P:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Store.KeyValueTuple`1.Value
member.summary: Value of the key value tuple.

member.name: P:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Store.KeyValueTuple`1.VersionSequenceNumber
member.summary: Version sequence number of the key value tuple. This is used to track the version of the key value tuple.
